title: Reasoning, Planning, Decomposition, and Verification · Eval Recall
summary: Prompt a difficult migration without exposing or demanding hidden reasoning. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a plan-execute-verify task contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.
Use planning before implementation when scope, dependencies, or user choices are materially uncertain.
Decompose work around independently verifiable outcomes and dependency order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Reasoning Selection · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Reasoning Selection without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.

content.text: Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Reasoning Selection, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a plan-execute-verify task contract.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Reliable reasoning requires the model to print every private thought. This fails the Reasoning Selection contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Agent Evaluation Guidance (2026) — Controls multi-turn and tool-using evaluation design for agentic prompts.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Uncertainty Report trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

stimulus.title: Uncertainty Report case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A confident answer should never mention uncertainty.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require bounded uncertainty, missing evidence, and blockers to remain visible. Artifact under review: a plan-execute-verify task contract.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Uncertainty Report, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Uncertainty Report applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require bounded uncertainty, missing evidence, and blockers to remain visible.
